title: mutation-result/v1
description: Raw, self-checking evidence from a mutation-testing run. Replaces summary-only claims: the document carries the raw counts, the denominator it declares, and the witnesses behind every claimed kill, so a consumer can recompute the rate instead of trusting it. A mutation run always states its scope; a scoped run is evidence about that scope only and never about the workspace.

schema.description: Contract discriminator. Consumers must reject any document whose schema they do not implement rather than best-effort parse it (E_UNSUPPORTED_SCHEMA).

repo.description: Repository this evidence was produced in. The repo name lives in the payload, never in the schema, so the schema file stays byte-identical across repos.

scope.description: What this run actually covered. Declared up front so a narrow run cannot later be cited as a broad claim.

claim.description: scoped: evidence about the named targets only. workspace: evidence about the whole workspace. A scoped run can never satisfy a readiness claim that requires workspace evidence.

ended_at.description: Completion instant, or null if the run never finished. Nullable on purpose: an unfinished run must be representable so it can be rejected with a precise reason (E_UNFINISHED) rather than a generic type error. A run that did not end has not measured anything.

shards.description: Every shard the run was split across. An incomplete shard means the population was never fully evaluated, so the counts are a floor, not a measurement (E_SHARD_INCOMPLETE).

counts.description: Raw population counts. These are the measurement; rate is derived from them and re-derived by the consumer.

unviable.description: Mutants that did not compile. Never part of any denominator: an unviable mutant tests nothing.

denominator.description: The formula this document declares for rate. Stated explicitly because the choice changes the number, and an undeclared denominator is how a rate becomes unfalsifiable.

rate.description: Declared kill rate. Recomputed from counts and denominator by the consumer; a declared rate that does not match is rejected (E_RATE_MISMATCH).

survivors.description: One classified record per distinct surviving mutant. The list length must equal counts.missed and mutant IDs must not repeat or also appear among kills: a summary count with fewer records or a repeated ID is not a taxonomy (E_SURVIVOR_COUNT_MISMATCH, E_DUPLICATE_MUTANT, E_MUTANT_POPULATION_OVERLAP).

kills.description: One witnessed record per distinct claimed kill. The list length must equal counts.caught and mutant IDs must not repeat or also appear among survivors: a kill without a witness is an assertion, not evidence (E_MISSING_WITNESS, E_DUPLICATE_MUTANT, E_MUTANT_POPULATION_OVERLAP).

taxonomy.description: Why this mutant survived. triage-pending is honest and allowed; omitting the field is not.

kill.description: A claimed kill and its two witnesses. Both directions are required: the mutant must fail a named test, and that same test must pass on unmutated HEAD. Without the second witness a permanently-red test would 'kill' every mutant.

mutant_fails_test.description: Witness that the mutant is detected: the named test fails with the mutant applied.

outcome.description: Pinned by const: this witness is only meaningful if the mutant made the test fail.

head_passes_test.description: Witness that the test is a real detector: the same test passes on unmutated HEAD.

outcome.description: Pinned by const: this witness is only meaningful if HEAD passes.

nullableTimestamp.description: An RFC 3339 UTC instant, or null when the event has not happened. Null is representable so it can be rejected by a named semantic rule rather than a generic type error.

resourceBudget.description: Declared resource ceilings the run was executed under. Required so a proof cannot be produced by a run that silently exhausted the host (the retrospective's ~40GB-RSS and 9,700-thread incidents).

isolated_target_dir.description: Per-run build directory. Must not be the shared/global target dir: a shared dir makes a run's inputs unattributable.

pid_task_max.description: Task/PID ceiling. The scarce resource in the cgroup-exhaustion incident; a memory cap alone was proven insufficient.

sha1.description: Full 40-character lowercase hex git object name. Abbreviated or uppercase SHAs are rejected: evidence must name an exact, unambiguous commit.

sourceRef.description: The exact tree this evidence was produced from.

tree_clean.description: False means uncommitted changes were present. Evidence from a dirty tree is not reproducible and is rejected (E_TREE_DIRTY).
